LAW AND MOTION 302, PLAINTIFF GCFS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS FAILED TO PRODUCE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE TO SUPPORT ITS FIRST CAUSE OF ACTION FOR BREACH OF CONTRACT. DEFENDANT'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ON GROUNDS OF AUTHENTICATION AND HEARSAY TO EXHIBIT 1 ARE SUSTAINED. OBJECTION TO EXHIBIT 2 IS SUSTAINED; NO FOUNDATION FOR THE CREDIT CARD STATEMENTS. PLAINTIFF THUS DID NOT MEET ITS BURDEN OF PRODUCTION TO MAKE A PRIMA FACIE SHOWING THAT THERE ARE NO TRIABLE ISSUES OF MATERIAL FACT. AGUILAR V. ATLANTIC RICHFIELD, 25 CAL. 4TH 826, 850 (2001). FURTHER, BECAUSE PLAINTIFF MOVED FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ONLY, THE COURT NEED NOT REACH THE SECOND CAUSE OF ACTION. THERE CAN BE NO SUMMARY ADJUDICATION WHERE THE NOTICE OF MOTION DID NOT REQUEST IT. THE PREVAILING PARTY IS REQUIRED TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WITH THE NAME AND ADDRESS OF THE PREVAILING PARTY'S COUNSEL OR THE PREVAILING PARTY IF PRO PER IN THE TOP LEFT OF THE FIRST PAGE OF PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE TENTATIVE RULING AND IS IN COMPLIANCE WITH CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE 437C(G). JUDGE: MARLA MILLER; CLERK: LESLEY FISCELLA, NOT REPORTED.

Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Continued To Jan-22-13 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

MOTION TO CONTINUE JURY TRIAL
Off Calendar Dec-20-2012 Text Ruling

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-13-2012 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: PLAINTIFF GCFS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is denied. Plaintiff has failed to produce admissible evidence to support its first cause of action for breach of contract. Defendant's evidentiary objections on grounds of authentication and hearsay to Exhibit 1 are sustained. Objection to Exhibit 2 is sustained; no foundation for the credit card statements. Plaintiff thus did not meet its burden of production to make a prima facie showing that there are no triable issues of material fact. Aguilar v. Atlantic Richfield, 25 Cal. 4th 826, 850 (2001). Further, because Plaintiff moved for summary judgment only, the court need not reach the second cause of action. There can be no summary adjudication where the notice of motion did not request it. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and is in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g). Judge: Marla Miller; Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Not reported.

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Dec-07-2012 Continued To Dec-13-12 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
